items.title: EPA Declines to Add Microplastics to Proposed UCMR 6

items.title: EPA Announces $450 Million Settlement with PFAS Manufacturer
items.abstract: On June 24, 2026, the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ), and the West Virginia Department of Environmental Protection (WV DEP) announced “the first comprehensive federal settlement with a major PFAS manufacturer, Chemours.”...By: Ber

items.title: Connecticut’s New PFAS Rules: What Businesses Need To Know About The Regulations And Covered Product
items.abstract: Connecticut has joined the growing roster of states cracking down on per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (“PFAS”), the so-called “forever chemicals” long prized for their water resistance, stain resistance, and non-stick performance....By: Benesch

items.title: California Lists Microplastics Under Safer Consumer Products Program
items.abstract: On June 18, 2026, the California Department of Toxic Substances Control (DTSC) issued a final rule adding microplastics to the Candidate Chemicals List (CCL) under the Safer Consumer Products (SCP) program. The rule takes effect on October 1, 2026. As discussed in our prior alert

items.abstract: On July 3, 2026, the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) published the Trump Administration’s 2026 Unified Agenda. The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency’s (EPA) Unified Agenda includes the following rulemakings under the Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA):...By: Bergeson &a

items.abstract: The US Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) recently released its proposed Sixth Unregulated Contaminant Monitoring Rule (UCMR 6), which would establish the parameters that water systems must monitor....By: ArentFox Schiff

items.title: Supreme Court Settles Circuit Split: Roundup Cannot Include Cancer Warning on Labels
items.abstract: The Environmental Protection Agency has consistently found no causal link between glyphosate-based pesticides and a heightened risk of cancer. As a result, EPA never required pesticide manufacturers to include cancer risk on glyphosate labels....By: Venable LLP

items.title: Forever Chemicals and the Family Farm: What NC Growers Need to Know About PFAS on Agricultural Land
items.abstract: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances, commonly known as PFAS or “forever chemicals,” are a large family of synthetic compounds that have been used since the 1940s in everything from nonstick cookware and food packaging to firefighting foam and industrial coatings....By: Ward and S
